# Step 1: Map ENSG ID to gene symbol using gene_info.parquet
import pandas as pd
from pathlib import Path

data_dir = Path("data/biomni_data/data_lake")
gene_info_path = data_dir / "gene_info.parquet"

print("Loading gene_info.parquet from:", gene_info_path)
gene_info = pd.read_parquet(gene_info_path)
print("gene_info columns:", gene_info.columns.tolist())

ensg_id = "ENSG00000143368"
# Common column names: check for ensembl gene id column
possible_cols = [c for c in gene_info.columns if "ensem" in c.lower() or "ensembl" in c.lower() or "ensembl_gene" in c.lower()]
print("Possible ENSEMBL columns:", possible_cols)

# Try several likely column names
match = None
for col in gene_info.columns:
    if gene_info[col].astype(str).str.contains(ensg_id).any():
        match = gene_info[gene_info[col].astype(str).str.contains(ensg_id)]
        ensembl_col = col
        break

print("Ensembl column used:", ensembl_col if match is not None else None)
print("Match rows count:", 0 if match is None else len(match))

if match is not None:
    print("Matching row(s):")
    print(match.head().to_dict(orient="records"))
    # Try to extract gene symbol from common columns
    symbol_cols = [c for c in gene_info.columns if c.lower() in ("symbol","gene_name","gene_symbol","hgnc_symbol","hgnc")]
    print("Potential symbol columns:", symbol_cols)
    gene_symbol = None
    for sc in symbol_cols:
        if sc in match.columns:
            gene_symbol = str(match.iloc[0][sc])
            break
    # Fallback: check common column names
    if not gene_symbol:
        for c in ["gene", "name", "Approved symbol", "hgnc_symbol"]:
            if c in match.columns:
                gene_symbol = str(match.iloc[0][c])
                break
    print("Mapped gene symbol:", gene_symbol)
else:
    gene_symbol = None
    print(f"No match found for {ensg_id} in gene_info.parquet")

# Save results
result_step1 = {"ensg_id": ensg_id, "gene_symbol": gene_symbol}
print("result_step1 =", result_step1)
</execute>

# Step 2: Resolve HPO IDs to phenotype names using hp.obo
from pathlib import Path
import re

hp_obo_path = Path("data/biomni_data/data_lake/hp.obo")
print("Loading hp.obo from:", hp_obo_path)

# Parse hp.obo to build mapping of HP:ID -> name
hp_map = {}
current_id = None
current_name = None
with open(hp_obo_path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
    for line in f:
        line = line.strip()
        if line == "[Term]":
            current_id = None
            current_name = None
        elif line.startswith("id: HP:"):
            current_id = line.split("id: ")[1].strip()
        elif line.startswith("name: "):
            current_name = line.split("name: ")[1].strip()
        elif line == "" and current_id and current_name:
            hp_map[current_id] = current_name
            current_id = None
            current_name = None
    # Final pass: in case file doesn't end with blank line
    if current_id and current_name:
        hp_map[current_id] = current_name

# Provided patient HPO IDs
patient_hpo = ["HP:0007646", "HP:0000347", "HP:0000175", "HP:0000365", "HP:0001172"]
resolved = {}
for h in patient_hpo:
    name = hp_map.get(h, None)
    resolved[h] = name

print("Resolved HPO terms:")
for h, n in resolved.items():
    print(h, "->", n)

result_step2 = {"patient_hpo": patient_hpo, "resolved": resolved}
print("result_step2 =", result_step2)
</execute><observation>Final output: {'disease_name': 'NAGER SYNDROME; NAGERS', 'OMIM_ID': '154400'}
